EQP-1: Provides a two-dimensional matrix f of size m×n.
EQP-2: Specifies a target value v to be searched within the matrix.
FUN-1: The system must determine a pair of indices (i, j) such that f(i, j) = v.
FUN-2: In the abstract model sm_m0, the final condition requires that i ∈ 0..m−1, j ∈ 0..n−1, and f(i, j) = v.
FUN-3: In the refinement sm_m1, the system introduces a Boolean variable searching that indicates whether the search is still in progress. When searching is true, the procedure begins at the bottom-right corner (i = m−1, j = n−1).
FUN-4: If searching is true and f(i, j) ≠ v with j = 0, then the algorithm updates i := i−1 and j := n−1, continuing the search in the previous row.
FUN-5: If searching is true and f(i, j) ≠ v with j ≠ 0, then the algorithm decrements j by 1 and continues searching.
FUN-6: The system introduces a Boolean variable found to represent whether the target value has been located. When found becomes true, it must hold that f(i, j) = v, and searching becomes false.
SAF-1: As long as searching is true, the value v, if present in the matrix, must remain within the unexplored submatrix [(0..i−1)×(0..n−1)] ∪ ({i}×(0..j)).
SAF-2: The algorithm ensures that no possible candidate position of v is skipped during the search.
SAF-3: When found is true, the condition f(i, j) = v guarantees that the abstract requirement of sm_m0 is satisfied.
DEG-1: If the value v is not present in the matrix, the algorithm will eventually exhaust all positions without success.
DEG-2: If m or n is 0 (empty matrix), the initialization cannot guarantee a valid starting index.
